assertionResults.title: Buyer Protection module is visible on transaction-relevant pages

steps.action: Log in as test user and open /my/tickets on a 375x812 viewport
steps.expected: Buyer Protection content is visible on the account trust surface
steps.observed: The My Tickets page rendered a trust block with '24/7 Support', 'Easy Refunds', '100% Guarantee', and a 'Buyer Protection' section reading '100% Money-Back Guarantee. Learn more about our buyer protection policy.'

steps.action: Open a directly relevant public trust surface at /football for copy comparison
steps.expected: Buyer Protection is also visible on browse/listing surfaces
steps.observed: The football listing page displayed 'Buyer Protection' with '100% Money-Back Guarantee. Learn more about our buyer protection policy.' plus 'Verified', 'Buyer Protected', and 'Secure Delivery' badges.

copyObservations: My Tickets: '24/7 Support' / 'Easy Refunds' / '100% Guarantee' / 'Buyer Protection' / '100% Money-Back Guarantee. Learn more about our buyer protection policy.'
Football: 'Buyer Protection' / '100% Money-Back Guarantee. Learn more about our buyer protection policy.' / 'Verified' / 'Buyer Protected' / 'Secure Delivery'
evidence.consoleErrors: none; browser showed only dev/performance warnings about smooth scrolling and Next/Image sizes
evidence.network: Authenticated account route loaded successfully at GET /my/tickets -> 200 document; no failing network requests observed during this assertion

assertionResults.title: Refund/support/guarantee messaging appears on account/order surfaces

steps.expected: Account surface shows refund/support/guarantee messaging with an assistance or policy route
steps.observed: My Tickets included '24/7 Support', 'Easy Refunds', '100% Guarantee', and a 'buyer protection policy' action.

steps.action: Open /my/orders to validate an order/history surface
steps.expected: Order/history surface also shows trust/support messaging
steps.observed: Order Records showed '24/7 Support', 'Easy Refunds', and 'Buyer Protection' with the same '100% Money-Back Guarantee. Learn more about our buyer protection policy.' copy beneath purchase history.

copyObservations: My Tickets: '24/7 Support' / 'Get help anytime with our dedicated customer support team' / 'Easy Refunds' / 'Full refund if your order doesn't arrive or isn't as described' / '100% Guarantee' / 'Every purchase is protected by our Buyer Protection program'
Order Records: '24/7 Support' / 'Easy Refunds' / 'Buyer Protection' / '100% Money-Back Guarantee. Learn more about our buyer protection policy.'

assertionResults.title: Trust copy is consistent across the app

steps.action: Compare trust naming on the public football page against account surfaces
steps.expected: Buyer Protection naming and trust terminology remain consistent across user-facing surfaces
steps.observed: The football page uses the badge label 'Buyer Protected' and trust badges 'Verified' / 'Secure Delivery', while account pages use 'Buyer Protection' headings and support/refund/guarantee cards without the 'Buyer Protected' badge wording.

steps.expected: Account and order surfaces use the same trust labels and claims
steps.observed: My Tickets adds a separate '100% Guarantee' heading with 'Every purchase is protected by our Buyer Protection program', whereas Order Records omits that heading and instead jumps directly from 'Easy Refunds' to 'Buyer Protection'.

copyObservations: Football: 'Buyer Protection' heading + 'Buyer Protected' badge + 'Verified' + 'Secure Delivery'
My Tickets: '24/7 Support' + 'Easy Refunds' + '100% Guarantee' + 'Buyer Protection'
Order Records: '24/7 Support' + 'Easy Refunds' + 'Buyer Protection' (no visible '100% Guarantee' heading)
evidence.network: Public and authenticated pages all loaded with 200 responses; failure is copy inconsistency rather than a routing/network issue

assertionResults.issues: Trust naming remains inconsistent across surfaces: public browse uses 'Buyer Protected' as a badge while account/order pages use 'Buyer Protection', and /my/tickets includes a '100% Guarantee' label that /my/orders does not mirror.

frictions.description: The /my/tickets page loaded successfully instead of the previously reported 404, but the seeded test account currently shows an empty-state tickets list, so trust validation relied on the empty-state trust module rather than populated owned-ticket cards.
frictions.resolution: Validated trust copy on /my/tickets and cross-checked against /my/orders and /football for consistency.

frictions.description: The 'buyer protection policy' control on /my/tickets appeared non-interactable in automation and did not reveal any additional modal or route during testing.
frictions.resolution: Recorded the visible trust copy and policy-action label as evidence without further interaction.

summary: Tested 3 account trust assertions: 2 passed (VAL-TRUST-007, VAL-TRUST-013) and 1 failed (VAL-TRUST-014). /my/tickets no longer 404s, buyer-protection and refund/support messaging are visible on account surfaces, but trust naming is still inconsistent between public and account/order pages.
